novel_features.name: Multi-site rank
novel_features.description: Rank a CSV of candidate sites by annual yield/kWp under the same system spec.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local store joining many PVGIS responses with shared system parameters; no PVGIS endpoint returns this in one call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent has a list of candidate roofs/sites and needs to recommend the highest-yield one without N round trips.

novel_features.name: Tilt/azimuth heatmap
novel_features.description: Compute a 2D grid of yields across tilt and azimuth, cache it, and serve future point queries from local interpolation.
novel_features.rationale: Requires caching the full grid in SQLite so later --tilt/--azimuth probes are offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before recommending a fixed-mount orientation when the user can choose tilt and azimuth independently.

novel_features.name: Database comparator
novel_features.description: Run the same site under PVGIS-SARAH3, PVGIS-NSRDB, and PVGIS-ERA5; report yield deltas.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join across parallel PVGIS calls keyed on the radiation database.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when assessing confidence intervals: how much do different databases disagree at this location?

novel_features.name: Yield delta vs reference
novel_features.description: Compare annual production at a baseline site against a target site under identical system specs.
novel_features.rationale: Requires both sites cached locally so the diff is computed without re-hitting the API.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to quantify how much a relocation or alternate roof loses or gains.

novel_features.name: TMY climate fingerprint search
novel_features.description: Rank cached TMYs by similarity to a target site's 12-month temperature and GHI signature.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cached TMY data for the candidate sites; PVGIS has no similarity endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to find analog climate sites for transfer-learning yield assumptions.

narrative.headline: The free, single-binary PVGIS CLI — JRC solar-radiation and PV-yield estimates with offline cache, multi-site batch, and agent-native JSON.
narrative.value_prop: Estimates monthly and hourly PV production, downloads Typical Meteorological Year (TMY) weather, fetches DEM-derived horizon profiles, and finds optimal tilt for any point covered by PVGIS-SARAH3, PVGIS-NSRDB, or PVGIS-ERA5. Adds offline SQLite caching, multi-site fan-out (`sites rank`), and a local 2D tilt/azimuth heatmap (`production sweep`) that no PVGIS endpoint returns directly.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the JRC API is reachable from this machine.
Monthly irradiance for a Milano-area site, fixed tilt 30 south.
Annual yield for a 5 kWp crystSi system at the same spot.
Sweep tilt 0-60 to find the production peak for a south-facing system.
Pull the full 8760-hour TMY; pipe through jq or save for downstream tools.

troubleshoots.symptom: All commands hang or return network errors
troubleshoots.fix: PVGIS occasionally has maintenance windows. Run `pvgis-pp-cli doctor` and retry. Use --base-url to pin to https://re.jrc.ec.europa.eu/api/v5_2 if v5_3 is degraded.
MRcalc requires --tilted=1 (or --selectrad=1) plus --tilt/--azimuth to compute the tilted plane. Without it only the horizontal plane is returned.
TMY returns 8760 hourly rows. Use `--select outputs.tmy_hourly[0]` to inspect one row, or pipe into the local SQLite store via `pvgis weather sync --lat --lon`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for pvgis-pp-cli when an agent or pipeline needs ground-truth solar-yield estimates from the JRC PVGIS service without subscribing to commercial APIs. Best when the workflow involves multiple candidate sites, repeated parameter sweeps, or downstream consumers (PVSyst, SAM, custom models) that want clean JSON or CSV.

recipes.title: Compare three rooftops under the same system spec
recipes.explanation: rooftops.csv must have lat,lon,label columns. Output ranks them by annual production.

recipes.title: Find the best tilt for a south-facing array
recipes.explanation: Returns just the winning tilt and its annual production.

recipes.title: Dump TMY climate as CSV for PVSyst
recipes.explanation: PVSyst and SAM consume hourly CSVs of T, GHI, DNI, DHI, wind.

recipes.title: Check terrain shading at a hilly site
recipes.explanation: Returns the 49-point DEM horizon in degrees; ingest into pvlib or visualize.

recipes.title: Compare databases for a North-American site
recipes.explanation: SARAH3 may not cover this longitude; NSRDB will. See how much they disagree where they overlap.
